transformation_type: acid-catalyzed rearrangement of propargylic alcohols to alpha,beta-unsaturated carbonyl compounds
mechanism_type: propargylic alcohol activation followed by allene/propargylic cation equilibration and hydration/tautomerization
common_conditions: protic or Lewis acid catalysis; Meyer-Schuster furnishes enals/enones while Rupe rearrangement of tertiary alcohols gives enones
description_short: Classical acid-mediated manifold converting propargylic alcohols into conjugated carbonyl compounds through rearrangement of the propargylic framework.

transformation_type: 1,4-conjugate addition of carbon or heteroatom nucleophiles to activated pi-systems
mechanism_type: nucleophilic conjugate addition to alpha,beta-unsaturated acceptors followed by protonation or further annulation
common_conditions: base-mediated or intramolecular conjugate addition; carbon and heteroatom nucleophiles both common
description_short: Foundational conjugate-addition reaction that forges carbon-carbon or carbon-heteroatom bonds at the beta-position of activated alkenes and alkynes.

transformation_type: enantioselective reduction of aldehydes and ketones with chiral Alpine-Borane reagents
mechanism_type: chiral borane hydride delivery to carbonyl groups through a stereodefined transition state
common_conditions: mild low-temperature borane reduction using chiral pinane-derived reducing agent
synonym_names: Midland Alpine-Borane Reduction (Midland Reduction)|Midland reduction|Alpine-Borane reduction
description_short: Asymmetric borane reduction that uses pinane-derived Alpine-Borane reagents to deliver enantioenriched alcohols from carbonyl precursors.

transformation_type: radical substitution of protonated electron-deficient heteroarenes
mechanism_type: generation of carbon-centered radicals followed by addition to protonated azaarenes and oxidative rearomatization
common_conditions: oxidative radical conditions in acidic media enabling functionalization of pyridines and related azaarenes
description_short: Radical-based alkylation or acylation of electron-poor heteroarenes that provides direct C-H functionalization under oxidative acidic conditions.

transformation_type: allylic sulfoxide to allylic alcohol rearrangement via sulfinate ester intermediates
mechanism_type: [2,3]-sigmatropic sulfur migration and thiophile-assisted reductive cleavage
common_conditions: sulfoxide formation then [2,3]-rearrangement with phosphite or other thiophile to reveal allylic alcohols
description_short: Stereospecific sulfur-based rearrangement manifold that converts allylic sulfoxides into allylic alcohols through reversible sulfinate/sulfoxide migration.
